revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{神社 |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}}

那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}}

那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]][[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:Ujiyoda Shrine]] [[sn:jiyoda Shrine]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}}

Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Qq 4]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]][[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|qq=|qid=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]][[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]][[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a [[:en:Setsumatsusha|sessha]] of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura [[:en:Human settlement|settlement]] in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style and is surrounded by a [[:en:Tamagaki|tamagaki]] as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]]. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River]], and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] }} [[ja:宇治山田神社]] {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
