It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlement}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki}} as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River}}. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ujiyoda Shrine (Q17194122)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|lt=Ujōda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|commons|Category:Ujiyōda-jinja|WD=Q17194122}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265}}; {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265}} || {{coord|34.468885|136.728451|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17194122]] Wikidata name: Ujōda-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlement}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki}} as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River}}. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ujiyoda Shrine (Q17194122)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|lt=Ujōda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|commons|Category:Ujiyōda-jinja|WD=Q17194122}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265}}; {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265}} || {{coord|34.468885|136.728451|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River}}. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ujiyoda Shrine (Q17194122)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|lt=Ujōda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|commons|Category:Ujiyōda-jinja|WD=Q17194122}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265}}; {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265}} || {{coord|34.468885|136.728451|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlement}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlement|1=Human settlement}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ujiyoda Shrine (Q17194122)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|lt=Ujōda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|commons|Category:Ujiyōda-jinja|WD=Q17194122|1=Ujiyoda Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265|1=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine}}; {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265|1=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine}} || {{coord|34.468885|136.728451|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Ujiyoda Shrine (Q17194122)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlement|1=Human settlement}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. {{Infobox religious building |名称= 宇治山田神社・那自賣神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Ujiyōda-jinja 01.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]中村町字西垣外918番地 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 7.9 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 42.3 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治山田神社：山田姫命
那自賣神社：大水上御祖命・御裳乃須蘇比賣命 |社格= 宇治山田神社：{{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（小）論社、{{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}摂社
那自賣神社：皇大神宮末社 |創建= 未詳 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]（[[10月22日]]） |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.468861|N|136.728417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Ujiyamada Shrine''' (宇治山田神社) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|WD=Q1071160|lt=sessha|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located on a small hill locally known as Okutama no Mori (興玉の森).Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.106 This article also covers '''Najime Shrine''' (那自賣神社), which shares the same site as Ujiyamada Shrine. == Overview == This section describes common features of both shrines. The shrines are situated at the southwestern edge of the Nakamura {{ill|Human settlement|en|Human settlement|WD=Q486972|lt=settlement|1=Human settlement}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}.Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai (1990): p.253 The shrine grounds cover 4 tan and 5 se (approximately 4,463 m2).Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.10 Both shrines are said to have been established by {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|弥姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|shaden|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}} is built in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style and is surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} as shown in the photo. There is no {{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saisenbako|WD=Q135491444|1=Saisenbako}}. The area around the shrine is covered with moss. == Ujiyamada Shrine == Ujiyamada Shrine Ranks 13th among the 33 sessha of the Inner Shrine. The {{ill|saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/saijin|qq=}} is Yodahime-no-Mikoto (山田比賣命), also known as Yamada-hime-no-Mikoto. She is considered a water deity, said to be the child of Ōminakami no Kami (大水上神). Together with {{ill|Katada Shrine|ja|堅田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katada Shrine|qq=}}, another sessha of the Inner Shrine, it is considered a candidate for the "Ōkunidama-hime Shrine" (大國玉比賣神社) mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}. == Najime Shrine == Najime Shrine Ranks 13th among the 16 massha of the Inner Shrine.Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.14 The enshrined deities are Ōminakami no Mioya no Mikoto (大水上御祖命) and Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto (御裳乃須蘇比賣命). Both are considered water guardians of the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}. Mimonosuso-hime no Mikoto is traditionally regarded as the spirit of Ōminakami no Kami.Ise City Tourism Association The main shrine has ceased to exist, and Najime Shrine is now co-located with Ujiyamada Shrine. == History == The Ōkunidama-hime Shrine mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} is considered a candidate for Ujiyamada Shrine, although it is not mentioned in the "Kōtai Jingū Gishiki-chō" and no shrine with the same name exists in modern Ise or Watarai District. In 1874, the shrine buildings of Ujiyamada Shrine, along with {{ill|Kamoshimo Shrine|ja|鴨下神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamoshimo Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Tsubura Shrine|ja|津布良神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubura Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|lt=Ōtsu Shrine|ja|大津神社 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Shrine (Ise)|qq=}} were reconstructed.Sakurai (1991): pp.280-281 Ujiyamada Shrine was rebuilt again in 1911 and underwent major repairs in 1959, with another relocation ceremony held in 2018. == Surroundings == Located within the Nakamura settlement. The entrance to the shrine is somewhat difficult to find despite Okutama no Mori being a noticeable hill. Nearby flows the {{ill|Isuzu River|en|Isuzu River|WD=Q6089522|lt=Isuzu River|1=Isuzu River}}, and beyond the Osoba Bridge is Isuzu Park. == References == == References == * Ujiyamada City Hall. "Ujiyamada City History, Volume I," 1929. * Sakurai, Katsunosuke. "Ise Jingū," Gakuseisha, 1969. * Shikinaisha Kenkyūkai, ed. "Shikinaisha Survey Report, Volume 6: Tōkaidō 1," Kōkagaku University Press, 1990. * Ise Bunka-sha, "Visiting All 125 Shrines of Ise," 2008. == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m03/0307.html 宇治山田神社（うじようだじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（那自賣神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|宇治山田神社|version=94246253|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ujiyōda-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Ujiyoda Shrine (Q17194122)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ise Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Ujiyoda Shrine|lt=Ujōda Shrine|ja|宇治山田神社|commons|Category:Ujiyōda-jinja|WD=Q17194122|1=Ujiyoda Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || part of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|WD=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} || {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265|1=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine}}; {{ill|Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|lt=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine|WD=Q135039265|1=Ohokunitamahimeno Shrine}} || {{coord|34.468885|136.728451|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181129 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17194122}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Ujiyōda-jinja]] [[ja:宇治山田神社]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
